name: viewport-minimal-qnode-inkdot
description: Viewport polish (2026-05-30) — qnode endpoints use the SAME junction affordance as regular endpoints, just grey-tinted. Earlier passes synthesized a separate nub at qnode endpoints to pad the gap left by the suppressed socket (bug-0051 follow-up). 2026-05-30 operator feedback: the synth nub introduced a third visual vocabulary nobody asked for; simpler to flip `socket.suppress` off and let qnodes share the regular endpoint's (nub, socket) cascade with grey as the only foreign-scope signal. Test: load workshop-palette demo, apply Minimal, assert ALL nub variant counts are zero (Minimal's `nubVariant: 'none'` means no nubs anywhere — qnodes included, no more synth). The `socket` channel grey-tints qnode sockets via `colorOverride`; that part is covered by the existing channel registry contract and not re-verified here.

commands: (async () => { const load = await window.__canaryLoadDemo('workshop-palette'); if (!load || load.ok !== true) throw new Error('failed to load workshop-palette demo: ' + JSON.stringify(load)); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500)); window.__canaryApplyProfile('minimal'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 600)); const counts = window.__canaryGetNubVariantCounts().value; if (!counts) throw new Error('getNubVariantCounts returned empty'); const total = Object.values(counts).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0); if (total !== 0) throw new Error('expected ALL nub variant counts == 0 in Minimal (nubVariant=none, no qnode synth-upgrade), got total=' + total + ' counts=' + JSON.stringify(counts)); return 'pass: every nub variant at 0 in Minimal — no synth, regular cascade applies'; })()
